extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50, condition: diabetes AND renal_impairment AND elderly). rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, condition: diabetes). No rule ever sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false, and each output has a single setter so priority/tie-breaking is irrelevant; the default false only applies when an output is left unset. The conjunction condition for rec_drug_a subsumes the diabetes condition for rec_drug_b, so whenever rec_a_from_conjunction fires, rec_b_from_trigger necessarily fires too. With diabetes=true, renal_impairment=true, elderly=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires -> rec_drug_a=true, and rec_b_from_trigger fires -> rec_drug_b=true. Both are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). seizure_history and low_ejection_fraction only influence rec_drug_c and are irrelevant (set false here). Therefore the property is violated.
